# Figure description: Схема_строения_каледонского_фундамента.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Общие_данные_для_всех_листов/Схема_строения_каледонского_фундамента.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **structural-geological interpretation map** (схема строения) of the Caledonian basement, compiled at a scale of 1:500,000. It is a tectonic-geological synthesis map combining geological mapping with geophysical interpretation results, showing the distribution of rock formations, structural zones, and fault systems within the crystalline basement.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The map covers the **western part of the Teniz Depression (Тенизская впадина)** in northern Kazakhstan, encompassing a large composite area assembled from multiple adjacent map sheets. The upper-right inset shows the **Kokshetav Block (Кокчетавская глыба)**. The map references drawing number **Чертёж №184** and inventory number **Инв. № 2633**. Geographic structural labels visible include:

- **КОКЧЕТАВСКАЯ ГЛЫБА** (Kokshetav Block) — upper right inset
- **АЛЕКСАНДРОВСКАЯ** — a structural zone or fault system trending NW-SE through the central portion
- **БАЯНАУЛЬСКАЯ** — another named tectonic zone in the lower-central area
- **КАРКАРАЛИНСКАЯ** — structural zone in the lower-right quadrant
- **ЖАКСЫ-ЖАЛГЫЗСКАЯ** (or similar) — structural element in the southwestern portion
- Several named fault zones and lineaments crossing the map diagonally
- Borehole/well reference annotations in the lower-left quadrant with depth notations (e.g., **Г-1, скв**, depth values in the range of ~1,500–6,000 m implied by notation style)
- The map is appended to the report of the **Западно-Тенизская ПСП** (West-Teniz Seismic Party)

---

## Key Content

The map uses a rich system of **fill patterns and symbols** to delineate distinct geological-tectonic domains within the Caledonian basement. Based on the legend (Условные обозначения), the following units and features are distinguished:

### Rock Formation Domains (patterned areas):

1. **+ + (cross pattern)** — Areas of predominant development of **alkaline granites of the Balmashin intrusive complex**
2. **Y Y (Y-symbol pattern)** — Areas of predominant development of **alkaline rocks of the Niyazov volcano-plutonic complex**
3. **+ × (mixed cross-X pattern)** — Areas of predominant development of **granitoids of the Krimkudunsk, Zerendinsky, and Stepnoy intrusive complexes**
4. **Г / г (Г-symbol hatching)** — Areas of predominant development of **siliceous-terrigenous, siliceous-terrigenous-basaltic formations of the Bretikovsky Series and Zerendinsky Series**, with intrusive analogues and minor formations; also **epifinal activation zones**
5. **Diagonal hatching (////)** — Areas of predominant development of **siliceous-terrigenous formations of the Ulytau Series; geosynclinal stage**
6. **Г Г (double Г pattern)** — Areas of predominant development of **andesite-basaltic formations of the Narymkol orogenic stage**
7. **о о (circle/dot pattern)** — Areas of predominant development of **terrigenous and terrigenous-siliceous formations of the Ordovician; geosynclinal stage of development**
8. **Г Vв (combined symbol)** — Areas of predominant development of **andesite-basaltic formations (geosynclinal stage)** with superimposed **liparite-andesite-terrigenous and calcareous formations of the Devonian (orogenic stage)**
9. **Г → (arrow-modified Г)** — Areas of **complex geological structure of epifinal activation** with superimposed complexes of the Narymkol orogenic stage and **Bayanaulsky and Bayandalsky folding**; also **contemporaneous by orogenic rocks**
10. **Г / (slash-modified Г)** — Areas of **complex geological structure of epifinal activation** with superimposed complexes of the orogenic stage of development

### Fault Systems (structural lines):

- **Zones of fault disturbances (Зоны разрывных нарушений)** — shown as broad shaded bands
- **Major faults possibly extending into the basement (Крупные разрывные нарушения, возможно, уходящие в верхнюю мантию)** — bold lines with distinctive hatching
- **Major faults (Крупные разрывные нарушения)** — heavy solid lines
- **Major faults from seismic data (Крупные разрывные нарушения по сейсмическим данным)** — dashed heavy lines
- **Minor faults (Мелкие разрывные нарушения)** — thin lines
- **Formation and intrusion contacts (Границы формаций и интрузий)** — medium lines
- **Possible erosion boundaries (Границы возможного астрорея [астроблемы?])** — curved dashed lines (notably a circular feature visible in the upper-right area, possibly a meteorite impact structure or dome)

### Geophysical Interpretation Results:
In the lower-right of the legend and annotated on the map:
- **Results of quantitative interpretation of local gravimetric anomalies**, showing:
  - **H₁** — depth to upper edge
  - **H₂** — depth to lower edge (visible thickness)
  - **25** — dip angle of boundary/fault
  - These are annotated at specific localities on the map

### Structural Pattern:
The map reveals a **mosaic of NW-SE and NE-SW trending fault zones** dividing the basement into discrete tectonic blocks. The dominant structural grain is **NW-SE (Caledonian trend)**. The Kokshetav Block in the upper right is characterized by **granitoid and alkaline intrusive domains**. The central and southwestern portions show complex interleaving of Ordovician geosynclinal sequences with later orogenic volcanic-sedimentary complexes.
